summary: This notebook implements an inference pipeline for BirdCLEF 2025, demonstrating how to load pre-trained EfficientNetV2-S models, convert raw audio recordings into normalized mel spectrograms, and generate a competition submission. It highlights a practical workflow for audio classification, including test-time augmentations and a lightweight temporal smoothing step to stabilize predictions across consecutive 5-second windows. Readers should take away the effectiveness of combining standard mel spectrogram extraction with EfficientNet backbones, along with the utility of simple post-processing for temporal consistency in audio tasks.
competition_flow: Loads pre-trained EfficientNetV2-S models and taxonomy data, processes test audio files into mel spectrograms, runs inference with optional TTA, applies temporal smoothing to the predictions, and exports a formatted submission CSV.
data_reading: Reads taxonomy CSV for class labels and sample submission template.
Glob iterates over .ogg files in the test soundscapes directory.
Loads audio waveforms using librosa.load at 32kHz sampling rate.
data_processing: Converts audio to mel spectrograms using librosa with N_FFT=1034, hop=64, n_mels=136, fmin=20, fmax=16000.
Handles NaN values, pads short segments to 5 seconds, normalizes to [0,1], and resizes to 256x256 via OpenCV.
Applies TTA during inference (horizontal flip for time shift, vertical flip for frequency shift).
ensembling: Averages predictions across multiple loaded model checkpoints and optionally applies TTA, followed by a weighted temporal smoothing filter (0.2-0.6-0.2) to reduce frame-to-frame noise in the final submission.
insights: Using FocalLossBCE from BirdCLEF 2024 produces strong OOF scores (~0.962) and transfers well to BirdCLEF 2025.
Converting audio to mel spectrograms and resizing to 256x256 provides a robust input format for EfficientNet backbones.
Simple temporal smoothing significantly stabilizes predictions across consecutive 5-second audio windows without complex modeling.
